title: Diversity and Inclusion Metrics Dashboard
dataSources: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees"` (employee_id, gender, ethnicity, age_band, role_level, department, hire_date, promotion_date)
spl:
index=business sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees" status="active"
| stats dc(employee_id) as headcount by gender, role_level
| eventstats sum(headcount) as level_total by role_level
| eval representation_pct=round(100*headcount/level_total, 1)
| sort role_level, gender
| table role_level, gender, headcount, representation_pct
description: Provides a real-time view of workforce composition by gender, ethnicity, age band, and role level — tracking representation trends over time and measuring progress against diversity goals. HR and executive leadership can see whether hiring and promotion practices are moving the needle on inclusion commitments.
value: Provides a real-time view of workforce composition by gender, ethnicity, age band, and role level — tracking representation trends over time and measuring progress against diversity goals. HR and executive leadership can see whether hiring and promotion practices are moving the needle on inclusion commitments.
implementation: (1) Import anonymised demographic data from HRIS; (2) handle self-reported data sensitively — include "Prefer not to say"; (3) schedule monthly; (4) track representation changes over time with timechart; (5) compare new hire diversity vs existing workforce diversity; (6) measure promotion rates by demographic group to identify glass ceiling patterns.
visualization: Stacked bar (representation by role level), Line chart (diversity trend over quarters), Table (representation vs targets), Single value (% representation by group).
knownFalsePositives: Metrics move with classification updates, M&A, outsourcing changes, or small populations where one hire changes percentages.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees"` (employee_id, gender, ethnicity, age_band, role_level, department, hire_date, promotion_date).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Import anonymised demographic data from HRIS; (2) handle self-reported data sensitively — include "Prefer not to say"; (3) schedule monthly; (4) track representation changes over time with timechart; (5) compare new hire diversity vs existing workforce diversity; (6) measure promotion rates by demographic group to identify glass ceiling patterns.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees" status="active"
| stats dc(employee_id) as headcount by gender, role_level
| eventstats sum(headcount) as level_total by role_level
| eval representation_pct=round(100*headcount/level_total, 1)
| sort role_level, gender
| table role_level, gender, headcount, representation_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Diversity and Inclusion Metrics Dashboard** — Provides a real-time view of workforce composition by gender, ethnicity, age band, and role level — tracking representation trends over time and measuring progress against diversity goals. HR and executive leadership can see whether hiring and promotion practices are moving the needle on inclusion commitments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees"` (employee_id, gender, ethnicity, age_band, role_level, department, hire_date, promotion_date). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: business; **sourcetype**: dbx:hris_employees. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=business, sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by gender, role_level** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for comparisons across those breakdowns for this use case).
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by role_level** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for comparisons across those breakdowns for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **representation_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Diversity and Inclusion Metrics Dashboard**): table role_level, gender, headcount, representation_pct

Step 3 — Validate
Compare diversity mix to HRIS and official DEI or compliance reports for the same definitions, regions, and snapshot dates. Follow legal review for any external disclosure.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (representation by role level), Line chart (diversity trend over quarters), Table (representation vs targets), Single value (% representation by group).
grandmaExplanation: We track diversity metrics carefully so we see progress in line with how HR and legal already report.
